This small park is surrounded by eclectic old architecture.  In the middle are benches and a children's playground.  It is left over from the 19th Century when this was a fashionable residential area.  It went into decline but now has been revived because of its charm.  Way more charming than the modern buildings that have sprung up in the nearby areas.  A couple of good restaurants here.

Had a few hours to relax midweek and enjoyed grabbing empanadas and ice tea from a local cafe and enjoy the afternoon. I felt very safe alone, the buildings along the park are very pretty and the park had nice play equipment.

This is a little oasis in the bustling SOMA area.  I have always liked a couple of the restaurants here.  And with the prosperity of the area, the bums have pretty much disappeared.  A place to sit in the middle of the square and a playground for the kids.  Lots of charm.
